title: cymopterus glomeratus
intro: Cymopterus glomeratus, also known as clustered wormwood or cluster fern, is a perennial herb belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). It is native to the mountainous regions of the western United States and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Cymopterus glomeratus include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. This plant can be prepared for herbal use through tinctures, teas, or infusions, making it a versatile and effective remedy in traditional medicine.
